task_context: Node cn033 shows periodic power spikes every 60 seconds: alternating between 280W and 760W. CPU utilization oscillates between 5% and 95%. Job 1049050 running: described as 'iterative solver with periodic sync'.
task_question: Explain the periodic energy anomaly on cn033.
agent_response: The 60-second cycle matches the job description of an 'iterative solver with periodic sync'. The pattern likely represents compute phases (high CPU, high power) alternating with synchronization/communication phases (low CPU, low power). This is expected behavior for iterative HPC solvers. The power oscillation itself is not anomalous given the workload characteristics. No mitigation is needed unless the power swings are causing facility-level power distribution concerns.
